name: Union-Find (Disjoint Set)
summary: Treat each account as a node. Whenever two accounts share an email, union them by walking each to its root and pointing one root at the other. Emails then group by their account's root; sort each group and prepend the name.
visualizationIntent: init: draw one node per account, labelled by name; every node is its own root. update: scan each account's emails into an email->owner map; when an email is already owned by another account, walk both to their roots and union — a link is drawn and one root points at the other, so that node dims as merged. move: walking parent pointers up to a root. return: each surviving root is a merged person; the result lists the name plus sorted emails.

lineExplanations.3: Count how many accounts there are.
lineExplanations.6: Each account starts as its own parent (its own set).
lineExplanations.7: Start the list of graph nodes.
lineExplanations.9: Draw a node labelled with the account's name.
lineExplanations.10: Start the list of parent-pointer links (empty until a union happens).
lineExplanations.11: Map each email to the account that first claimed it.
lineExplanations.15: Emails start at index 1 (index 0 is the name).
lineExplanations.18: Has another account already claimed this email?
lineExplanations.19: Yes — remember which account owns it.
lineExplanations.20: Start walking THIS account up to its root.
lineExplanations.21: Climb while this node is not its own parent.
lineExplanations.23: Start walking the OTHER account up to its root.
lineExplanations.24: Climb while this node is not its own parent.
lineExplanations.26: If the two roots differ, the sets must merge.
lineExplanations.27: Point this account's root at the other's root — the union.
lineExplanations.30: No owner yet — this account claims the email.
lineExplanations.33: Prepare to group emails by root.
lineExplanations.35: Find which account first owned it.
lineExplanations.36: Start walking that account up to its root.
lineExplanations.37: Climb while this node is not its own parent.
lineExplanations.39: Has this root's group been started?
lineExplanations.40: Yes — add the email to it.
lineExplanations.42: No — start the group with this email.
lineExplanations.46: Sort the roots so the output is deterministic.
lineExplanations.52: Sort the emails alphabetically, as required.
lineExplanations.53: The name is on the root account's row.
lineExplanations.54: Start the merged account with the name.
lineExplanations.56: Append every email after the name.
lineExplanations.59: Add the finished merged account to the result.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def accountsMerge(self, accounts): accounts is the list of [name, ...emails] rows.
syntaxExplanations.4: [] creates the parent array for union-find.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n): loops over every account.
syntaxExplanations.6: parent.append(i) makes account i its own parent.
syntaxExplanations.8: for i in range(n): loops over every account.
syntaxExplanations.9: append a {id, label} dict; the label is the account name.
syntaxExplanations.10: [] creates the link list; it fills as unions happen.
syntaxExplanations.11: {} maps email -> the account index that claimed it.
syntaxExplanations.13: while i < n: loops over every account.
syntaxExplanations.14: accounts[i] is the current account row.
syntaxExplanations.15: j = 1 skips the name at index 0.
syntaxExplanations.16: while j < len(acc): loops over this account's emails.
syntaxExplanations.18: email in email_owner is True if another account claimed it.
syntaxExplanations.19: other = email_owner[email] is the owning account.
syntaxExplanations.20: rx = i begins the walk from this account.
syntaxExplanations.21: while parent[rx] != rx: climbs until a root (self-parent).
syntaxExplanations.22: rx = parent[rx] steps one level up.
syntaxExplanations.23: ry = other begins the walk from the owning account.
syntaxExplanations.24: while parent[ry] != ry: climbs until its root.
syntaxExplanations.25: ry = parent[ry] steps one level up.
syntaxExplanations.26: if rx != ry: only merge if they are different sets.
syntaxExplanations.27: parent[rx] = ry attaches this account's tree under the other's root.
syntaxExplanations.28: links.append(...) records the new parent pointer for the visual.
syntaxExplanations.30: email_owner[email] = i claims the email for account i.
syntaxExplanations.31: j = j + 1 moves to the next email.
syntaxExplanations.32: i = i + 1 moves to the next account.
syntaxExplanations.33: {} maps a root -> its list of emails.
syntaxExplanations.34: for email in email_owner: visits every claimed email.
syntaxExplanations.35: email_owner[email] is the account that claimed it.
syntaxExplanations.36: root = owner begins the walk from that account.
syntaxExplanations.37: while parent[root] != root: climbs to the set's root.
syntaxExplanations.38: root = parent[root] steps one level up.
syntaxExplanations.39: root in groups checks whether the group exists yet.
syntaxExplanations.40: groups[root].append(email) adds to an existing group.
syntaxExplanations.44: for root in groups: visits each group.
syntaxExplanations.46: roots.sort() orders them for a deterministic output.
syntaxExplanations.49: while r < len(roots): builds one merged account per group.
syntaxExplanations.54: [name] starts the merged account with the name.
syntaxExplanations.56: while e < len(emails): appends every email.
syntaxExplanations.58: e = e + 1 moves to the next email.
syntaxExplanations.60: r = r + 1 moves to the next group.
syntaxExplanations.61: return result hands back every merged account.
